title: Reproducibility Scripts for Complex-Valued Chemometrics (ILS, PCR, PLS, Nested MCCV)
author: Mayerhöfer, Thomas
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains reproducibility scripts and example data associated with the manuscript: 
 Mayerhöfer, T. G.,  Complex-valued Chemometrics for Analyzing Absorbance and Raman Spectra  (currently under peer review). 
 The repository provides complete implementations of the chemometric methods described in the manuscript, including: 
 
 
 Complex-valued Inverse Least Squares (ILS) with Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV) 
 
 
 KK-constrained and standard Principal Component Regression (PCR) 
 
 
 Transpose-only complex Partial Least Squares (PLS) variants 
 
 
 A nested Monte-Carlo cross-validation (MCCV) framework for rigorous comparison between imaginary-only and full complex ILS 
 
 
 All validation procedures are implemented with strict TRAIN/TEST separation. Model parameters, principal components, regression coefficients, peak selections, and optional concentration corrections are recomputed within each cross-validation fold. No test data are used during model construction. 
 The repository includes: 
 
 
 Mathematica notebooks implementing each method 
 
 
 A nested MCCV evaluation script 
 
 
 Example spectral data (complex spectral data of binary mixtures) 
 
 
 Corresponding concentration data 
 
 
 The code is deterministic (fixed random seed where applicable) and uses numerically stable solvers (QR/Cholesky with SVD fallback). No external toolboxes are required. 
 This archive is intended to enable independent verification and reproduction of the numerical results reported in the manuscript. 
 Upon publication of the manuscript, the full citation and DOI will be added to this record.
